content_id:
Orang-orang yang hadir hendaknya mendorong orang yang sedang sekarat untuk mengucapkan kalimat syahadat mengikuti mereka, hingga ia meninggal dalam keadaan menegaskan keesaan Allah (Tauhid). Baik Abu Sa'id al-Khudri maupun Abu Hurairah meriwayatkan sabda Nabi (shallallahu alaihi wa sallam): "Talqinkanlah orang yang sedang sekarat di antara kalian dengan kalimat 'laa ilaaha illallaah', dan barangsiapa yang akhir ucapannya sebelum meninggal adalah 'laa ilaaha illallaah', maka ia akan masuk surga suatu hari nanti, meskipun sebelum itu ia pernah ditimpa azab." Hadis Nabi ini tidak sekadar berarti bahwa syahadat cukup disebutkan oleh orang lain di hadapan orang yang sekarat. Orang yang sekarat itu sendiri harus diajarkan untuk mengucapkan syahadat, sebagaimana jelas dari sabda Nabi: "barangsiapa yang akhir ucapannya adalah 'laa ilaaha illallaah'." Hadis yang diriwayatkan oleh Anas berikut ini menunjukkan praktik Nabi dalam hal tersebut. Hadis ini diriwayatkan dalam Sahih Muslim, Sunan Abu Dawud, Sunan Ibn Majah, Mishkat Al-Masabih, dan Riyadhus Shalihin.

Anas bin Malik meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu alaihi wa sallam) mengunjungi seorang lelaki dari kalangan Anshar dan berkata kepadanya, "Wahai Paman, ucapkanlah: laa ilaaha illallaah." Lelaki itu bertanya, "Apakah saya ini paman dari pihak ibu atau paman dari pihak ayah?" Nabi menjawab, "Sesungguhnya engkau adalah paman dari pihak ayah." Lalu lelaki itu bertanya lagi, "Apakah lebih baik bagiku untuk mengucapkan: laa ilaaha illallaah?" Nabi menjawab, "Ya." Para Muslim juga dianjurkan untuk hadir ketika orang-orang non-Muslim sedang sekarat, untuk menyampaikan Islam kepada mereka dengan harapan mereka menerima Islam sebelum meninggal. Agar penerimaan Islam di saat seperti ini bermanfaat, ia harus didasarkan pada ilmu, diucapkan dengan tulus dan keyakinan, serta terjadi sebelum dimulainya saat-saat sakaratul maut yang sebenarnya. Hal ini tampak dari upaya Nabi untuk mendapatkan persaksian paman beliau, Abu Thalib, atas keesaan Allah ketika paman beliau berada di ranjang kematiannya.

Anas juga meriwayatkan bahwa ketika seorang pemuda Yahudi yang biasa melayani Nabi (shallallahu alaihi wa sallam) jatuh sakit, Nabi mengunjunginya. Beliau duduk di sisi kepalanya dan berkata, "Masuk Islamlah." Pemuda itu memandang ayahnya yang berada di dekatnya, dan ayahnya berkata, "Taatilah Abul-Qasim." Maka pemuda itu pun masuk Islam, dan Nabi meninggalkan tempat itu seraya mengucapkan, "Segala puji bagi Allah yang telah menyelamatkannya dari Neraka." Ketika pemuda itu meninggal, Nabi bersabda, "Shalatkanlah sahabat kalian ini."
content_en:
Those people who are present should encourage the dying person to repeat after them the Declaration of Faith (Shahaadah) until he or she passes away confirming AllaahвҖҷs unique oneness (tawhid). Both Aboo Sa вҖҳeed al-Khudree and Aboo Hurayrah quoted the Prophet (r) as saying, ))п»Ёпұ пә”п№¶о  пәЁп№¶Щ„ЩҺ пәҚп»ҹп№ҫпә о   пә•Щҗ пә©о ўп»ӯо   пәҚп»ҹп№ҫп»Өо   п»ӢЩҗп»Ёп№ҫпә©о Ў пәҮЩҗп»»пұ  пәҚп»ҹп» пұ п»Әо   п»ңп№¶п»јп№¶п»ӨЩҗп»ӘЩҗ п»»п№¶ пәҮЩҗп»ҹп№¶п»Әо Ў пәҒЩҺпәЁп№¶пәӯо   п»ңп№¶пәҺп»Ҙо ўп»Ҙо  п»Өо   п»ӯо Ў пәҮЩҗп»»пұ  пәҚп»ҹп» пұ п»Әо   п»»п№¶ пәҮЩҗп»ҹп№¶п»Әо ўпәҳп№¶пәҺп»ңп№ёп»Ўо ўп»ӯо   п»ҹп№¶п»ҳпұўп»Ёп№ёп»ӯпәҚ п»Ө о Ўп»Әо  пәҺпә’о  пәҺ пәғЩҺпәјо   п»Өо  Щ„ЩҺ пә«п№¶п»ҹЩҗп»ҷо ў п»—п№¶пә’о Ўп»Әо  пәҺпә’о   пәғЩҺпәјо ўпәҮЩҗп»Ҙо  пәӯЩҗШҢ п»ӯо ўп»«о Ё пәҚп»ҹпә©о  пәҺ п»ӨЩҗп»Ҙо Јп»Өо ўп»ӯо  п»ҙ (( вҖңPrompt your dying ones (to say): laa ilaaha il-lal-laah1 and whoeverвҖҷs last words before dying are: laa ilaaha il-lal-laah, will enter paradise one day, even if he is afflicted before that by punishment.вҖқ This prophetic statement does not simply mean that the Shahaadah should be mentioned by others in the presence of the one who is dying. The dying person, himself or herself, should be instructed to say the Shahaadah, as is evident from the ProphetвҖҷs statement вҖңwhoeverвҖҷs last words are: laa ilaaha il-lal-laahвҖқ. The following hadith narrated by Anas indicates the ProphetвҖҷs practise in this regard: 1 Sahih Muslim, vol.2, p.435, no. 1996, Sunan Abu Dawud, vol.2, p.887, no.3111, Sunan Ibn-iMajah, vol.2, p.365, no.1444, Mishkat Al-Masabih, vol.1, p.337 and Riyadh-us-Saleeheen, vol.2, p.474, no. 918. 2 This addition is only found in sahih Ibn Hibbaan and it is authenticated in Irwaa al-Ghaleel, vol.3, p.150.

Щҗп»ӯЩ„ЩҺ пәҚп»ҹп» пұ п»Әо Ўпәҙо   пәӯо ЁпәҺп»ҹЩҗп»ҷЩҚ пәғЩҺп»Ҙо  п»ҘЩҗ п»Өо ў пәғЩҺп»Ёп№¶пәұЩҚ пә’о ўп»Ҙо  п»Ӣ r пәҺпәӯЩҗ п»“п№¶п»ҳп№¶пәҺЩ„ЩҺо   пәҚп№ҫп»·ЩҺп»Ёп№ҫпәјо  п»јп№° п»ӨЩҗп»Ҙо Ўпә о   пәӯо  пәҺпә©о   п»Ӣ )) : пәҮЩҗп»»пұ  о  пәҺ пәЁп№¶пәҺЩ„ЩҸ п»—п№ёЩ„Щ’ п»»п№¶ пәҮЩҗп»ҹп№¶п»Әо  п»ҙ о ЎпәҚп»ҹп» пұ п»Ә (( п»“п№¶п»ҳп№¶пәҺЩ„ЩҺ : Шҹ п»“п№¶п»ҳп№¶пәҺЩ„ЩҺо «п»Ўо   п»Ӣо ўпәғЩҺпәЁп№¶пәҺЩ„ЩҢ пәғЩҺп»Ў : )) п№¶п»» Щ„Щ’ пәЁп№¶пәҺЩ„ЩҢ о   пә’ (( п»—п№¶пәҺЩ„ЩҺ : Шҹ о Ў пәҮЩҗп»»пұ  пәҚп»ҹп» пұ п»Әо   пәғЩҺп»—п№ёп»ӯЩ„ЩҺ п»»п№¶ пәҮЩҗп»ҹп№¶п»Әо ў п»ҹЩҗп»І пәғЩҺп»Ҙо Өпәӯо ўп»“п№¶пәЁп№¶п»ҙ о ©п»“п№¶п»ҳп№¶пәҺЩ„ЩҺ пәҚп»ҹп»Ёпұ пә’Щҗп»І )) : r о ўп»Ўо  п»Ёп№¶п»Ң (( Anas ibn Maalik reported that the Messenger of Allaah (r) visited a man from among the Ansaar and said to him,вҖңO Uncle, say : laa ilaaha il-lal-laah. The man asked, вҖңAm I a maternal uncle or a paternal uncle?вҖқ and the Prophet (r) replied,вҖңIndeed you are paternal.вҖқ The man then asked, вҖңIs it better for me to say : laa ilaaha il-lal-laah? The Prophet (r) replied, вҖҳYes.вҖҷвҖқ3 Muslims are also encouraged to be present when non-Muslims are dying in order to present Islaam to them, in the hope that they would accept Islaam prior to their death. For the acceptance of Islaam at this point to be of any benefit, it must be based on knowledge,4 it must be expressed sincerely5 and with certainty,6 and it must take place before the actual throes of death begin.7 This point is evident in the ProphetвҖҷs (r) attempt to get his uncle Aboo Taalib to bear witness to AllaahвҖҷs oneness when the latter was on his deathbed.8 Aboo Taalib knew about Allaah, Islaam, and the Prophet (r) very well. Similarly, the following incident involving the ProphetвҖҷs servant is one of a person who knew about Allaah and the Prophet (r). 3 Collected by Ahmad and authenticated in al-Fat-h ar-Rabbaanee, vol.7, p.57, no.34 as well as in Ahkaamul-JanaaвҖҷiz, p.11. } ЩҸп»“ЩҺпәҺп»ӢЩ’п» ЩҺп»ӨЩҸп»®Ш§Щ’ ШЈЩҺп»§пұ п®«ЩҸ п»ө ШҘЩҗп»ҹЩҺп®«ЩҺ ШҘп»»пұ  Ш§п·І { вҖң Know that there is no god worthy of worship besides Allaah.вҖқ Chapter Muhammad, (47):19. } ЩҺЩҲЩҺп»ЈЩҺпәҺ ШЈЩҸп»ЈЩҗпә®ЩҸЩҲШ§Щ’ ШҘп»»пұ  п»ҹЩҗпҜҝЩҺп»ҢЩ’пә’ЩҸпәӘЩҸЩҲШ§Щ’ Ш§п·ІЩҺ п»ЈЩҸпәЁЩ’п» ЩҗпәјЩҗпҜҝп»ҰЩҺ п»ҹЩҺп®«ЩҸ Ш§п»ҹпәӘпұўпҜҫп»Ұ { вҖң The were only commanded to worship Allaah, making the religion sincere for Him alone.вҖқ Chapter al-Bayyinah, (98):5. 6 Aboo Hurayrah reported AllaahвҖҷs Messenger as saying, вҖңI testify that there is no god worthy of worship but Allaah and that I am the Messenger of Allaah. Any servant who meets Allaah with those two [testimonies], having no doubt in them, will enter paradise.вҖқ (Sahih Muslim, vol.1, p.20, no.41) 7 The Prophet (r) was reported by вҖҳAbdullaah ibn вҖҳUmar to have said, вҖңAllaah, Most Great and Glorious, will accept His servantвҖҷs repentance until the throes of death begin.вҖқ (Collected by atTirmithee and Ibn Maajah and authenticated in sahih Sunan Ibn Maajah, vol.2, p.418, no.3430. See also Riyadh-us-Saleheen, vol.1, p.12, no.18, for an English translation of this hadith). 8 See Sahih Muslim, vol.1, p.18, no.36.

The Prophet (r) sat by his head and said to him, вҖңAccept Islaam.вҖқ The boy looked at his father who was near him and the man said, вҖңObey Abul-Qaasim.вҖқ So the boy accepted Islaam and the Prophet (r) left saying, вҖңAll praise is due to Allaah who saved him from the Fire.вҖқ 9 (When the boy died the Prophet (r) said, вҖңMake salaah for your comrade.вҖқ)10
